evals.prompt: I have a CSV file at data/reviews.csv with columns 'review_text' and 'star_rating'. I want to automatically tag each review as positive, negative, or neutral sentiment. I have about 200 rows, 50 of which I've already manually labeled in a 'sentiment' column. Can you build me a classifier that uses those labeled ones to learn and then tags the rest?
evals.expected_output: A Python script that loads the CSV, splits labeled data into train/dev, defines a DSPy signature with Literal sentiment categories, evaluates baseline, optimizes with BootstrapFewShot, and batch-processes the unlabeled rows. Should save the optimized model.

assertions.name: uses_dspy_signature
assertions.description: Defines a dspy.Signature class for the classification task

assertions.name: uses_literal_types
assertions.description: Uses Literal type to constrain sentiment to valid categories

assertions.name: loads_csv_data
assertions.description: Reads from CSV and creates dspy.Example objects

assertions.name: splits_labeled_unlabeled
assertions.description: Separates labeled rows for training from unlabeled ones for inference

assertions.name: includes_evaluation
assertions.description: Measures baseline accuracy with dspy.evaluate before optimizing

assertions.name: includes_optimization
assertions.description: Uses a DSPy optimizer (BootstrapFewShot or MIPROv2) to improve accuracy

assertions.name: batch_processes_unlabeled
assertions.description: Applies the optimized model to classify the unlabeled rows

evals.prompt: We get about 500 support tickets a day and need to route them to the right team — billing, technical, account, or security. The tricky part is some tickets are ambiguous and we don't want to misroute those. Can you build something that sorts them but flags the ones it's not sure about so a human can review those instead?
evals.expected_output: A Python script with a DSPy signature that classifies tickets AND outputs a confidence score. Should include a threshold check that flags low-confidence items for human review rather than auto-routing them. Should use ChainOfThought for reasoning.

assertions.name: uses_dspy_signature
assertions.description: Defines a dspy.Signature class for ticket routing

assertions.name: has_four_categories
assertions.description: Includes billing, technical, account, and security as routing categories

assertions.name: includes_confidence
assertions.description: Outputs a confidence score alongside the category

assertions.name: has_threshold_logic
assertions.description: Implements a threshold check that flags low-confidence items for human review

assertions.name: uses_chain_of_thought
assertions.description: Uses ChainOfThought module for reasoning before classification

evals.prompt: I'm building a voice AI agent with LiveKit and I have a bunch of call transcript JSON files in a transcripts/ folder. Each file has a 'segments' array with 'text' fields. I want to classify each call by topic — sales_inquiry, support_issue, billing_question, partnership, or other. No labeled data yet, I just want to get something working and iterate from there.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that loads LiveKit transcript JSONs, extracts text from segments, defines a DSPy classifier with the specified categories including 'other' as a catch-all, and demonstrates how to run it on the transcripts. Since there's no labeled data, should either suggest labeling a small set or use ai-generating-data. Should show how to save results.

assertions.name: uses_dspy_signature
assertions.description: Defines a dspy.Signature class for transcript classification

assertions.name: loads_json_transcripts
assertions.description: Reads JSON files and extracts text from the segments array

assertions.name: handles_no_labeled_data
assertions.description: Addresses the cold-start problem — suggests labeling, synthetic data, or a zero-shot approach

assertions.name: classifies_whole_calls
assertions.description: Classifies at the call level, not individual transcript segments
